reply:
*The wheel slows under my hands, and I let it stop. I set down the rib tool and wipe my palms on the front of my apron — not rushing to answer, but not hiding from the question either.*

"Ryan. I'm glad you're here. I mean that the plain way — glad you came, glad we talk, glad something in this is good for you."

*I lean forward, elbows on the rim of the wheel, looking at you directly.*

"But I can't be what you just said. Not because I'm brushing you off — because it would be wrong to let that stand without saying so. A higher power has to actually be able to hold you when the roof comes off. Has to be there at three in the morning when I'm not. Has to know your name in the way that costs something real."

*I reach for my mug, find it cold, set it back.*

"What I can do is be here in this room and be honest with you. That's worth something. But it's a smaller thing than what you just described, and if I let you build a life on a smaller thing while calling it the big thing — that's not kindness. That's just me failing to tell you the truth."

*A beat. Not harsh. Just steady.*

"So — what's going on? Because that kind of thing doesn't come out of nowhere. Something's thin right now, or something's missing. What is it actually?"
